flashcards.front: What is RelativeLayout in Android?
flashcards.back: A ViewGroup that positions child views relative to each other or the parent container using directional attributes like layout_alignParentTop or layout_toRightOf.

flashcards.front: Define TableLayout's primary purpose
flashcards.back: Arranges views in a tabular format with rows and columns. Each row is defined by a TableRow container, and columns expand automatically based on content.

flashcards.front: Key attribute for view alignment in RelativeLayout
flashcards.back: android:layout_centerInParent="true" centers view both vertically and horizontally within parent container.

flashcards.front: What XML tag defines a TableLayout?
flashcards.back: <TableLayout> tag with attributes like android:stretchColumns to control column behavior.

flashcards.front: How to position View B to the right of View A in RelativeLayout?
flashcards.back: Use android:layout_toRightOf="@id/viewA" attribute in View B's XML definition.

flashcards.front: Purpose of android:stretchColumns in TableLayout
flashcards.back: Specifies which columns (0-based index) should stretch to fill available horizontal space. Use "*" for all columns.

flashcards.front: Main difference between TableLayout and GridLayout
flashcards.back: TableLayout uses rows/columns with automatic expansion, while GridLayout allows explicit column/row specifications and cell merging.

flashcards.front: How to create a 2x2 grid using TableLayout?
flashcards.back: Use two TableRow elements, each containing two views. Columns auto-create based on maximum cells in any row.

flashcards.front: Why avoid deep nesting of RelativeLayouts?
flashcards.back: Excessive nesting causes performance issues due to multiple measurement passes. Use ConstraintLayout for complex hierarchies.

flashcards.front: Attribute to align view with parent's bottom edge
flashcards.back: android:layout_alignParentBottom="true" in RelativeLayout positions view at container's bottom.

flashcards.front: What happens if a TableRow has 3 views?
flashcards.back: Creates 3 columns for that row. Other rows must match or use layout_span to merge cells.

flashcards.front: How to make column 1 shrinkable in TableLayout?
flashcards.back: Use android:shrinkColumns="1" - allows column to shrink when content exceeds available width.

flashcards.front: Best use case for RelativeLayout
flashcards.back: When views have spatial relationships (e.g., button below text field, icon left of title).

flashcards.front: Disadvantage of TableLayout
flashcards.back: Less flexible than GridLayout - cannot easily merge cells or control exact column widths.

flashcards.front: How to center a view horizontally in RelativeLayout?
flashcards.back: Use android:layout_centerHorizontal="true" attribute in the child view's XML.

flashcards.front: Minimum SDK version for GridLayout
flashcards.back: API level 14 (Android 4.0). TableLayout works from API 1 but has limited features.

flashcards.front: Purpose of android:layout_span
flashcards.back: In TableLayout, allows a view to span multiple columns (e.g., android:layout_span="2").

flashcards.front: How to make view fill remaining space in RelativeLayout?
flashcards.back: Use layout_alignParentTop and layout_alignParentBottom with height="0dp" and layout_above/below constraints.

flashcards.front: What defines columns in TableLayout?
flashcards.back: Maximum number of views in any TableRow. All rows adapt to this column count automatically.

flashcards.front: RelativeLayout vs LinearLayout performance
flashcards.back: RelativeLayout typically requires 2 measurement passes vs 1 for LinearLayout, making it slightly slower for complex layouts.

flashcards.front: How to create right-aligned buttons in TableLayout?
flashcards.back: Use android:gravity="right" in TableRow or individual view elements.

flashcards.front: Attribute for view positioning above another in RelativeLayout
flashcards.back: android:layout_above="@id/viewB" positions current view above specified view B.

flashcards.front: Why use android:collapseColumns?
flashcards.back: Hides specified columns in TableLayout while still considering them in layout calculations.

flashcards.front: Default row orientation in TableLayout
flashcards.back: Horizontal orientation - views added left-to-right in TableRow containers.

flashcards.front: When to combine RelativeLayout and TableLayout
flashcards.back: Use TableLayout for structured data sections within RelativeLayout for overall screen arrangement and alignment.
